title: Effective Connectivity of Functionally Anticorrelated Networks Under Lysergic Acid Diethylamide.

assets.abstract: BACKGROUND: Classic psychedelic-induced ego dissolution involves a shift in the sense of self and a blurring of the boundary between the self and the world. A similar phenomenon is identified in psychopathology and is associated with the balance of anticorrelated activity between the default mode network, which directs attention inward, and the salience network, which recruits the dorsal attention network to direct attention outward. METHODS: To test whether changes in anticorrelated networks un

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Self-dissolution — ego dissolution, DMN decoupling, depersonalization, nondual awareness.
